hard_stop_loss.definition: P&L <= 0 and exit tag contains Stop, excluding the earlier more-specific categories. Represents trades that made some progress but still reverted into a stop-based loss.
hard_stop_loss.theme: moderate progress reversal before durable protection
hard_stop_loss.next_step_posture: highest-priority unresolved weakness; if iteration continues, prefer a new mid-trade protection ratchet between BE activation and full profit-lock activation

equivalence_assessment.equivalence_reason: The strongest ORB hold ratchet variant reproduces the prior ORB re-entry branch on the decision metrics (pnl delta, improved folds, improved years, saved losses, clipped winners), so this should not be treated as a genuinely new post-v26 mechanism.

method: Alpaca-backed local feasibility test for an ORB hold ratchet on top of v26. Once a breakout has made enough favourable progress, the branch promotes a structural stop floor near the ORB breakout hold zone so moderate-progress reversals are cut earlier without relying on raw ORB re-entry exits.

structural_conclusion.research_interpretation: This branch promotes the ORB breakout hold zone into a persistent stop floor after enough progress, but the strongest Alpaca result now appears behaviorally equivalent to the earlier ORB re-entry branch.
structural_conclusion.next_step_rule: Do not build a Blueshift evaluator if the strongest ORB hold ratchet result is equivalent to the earlier ORB re-entry branch; only reopen it if later evidence shows meaningfully different QC or local behavior.
